metric_value: COBOL, BASIC, C++, Smalltalk, Java (upcoming), Generator
notes: VisualAge family spans multiple 3GLs under common GUI environment. Key competitive advantage for IBM installed base.

metric_value: upcoming 1997 release
notes: VisualAge for Java will build HTML pages, connect web apps to network services, dynamically generate HTML, drag-and-drop visual builds.

metric_value: OS/2, AIX, Windows NT, HP-UX servers; OS/2, Win3.1, AIX, NT clients
notes: FlowMark runtime support covers major enterprise platforms of the era.

metric_value: platform-independent spec with multi-environment instantiation
notes: Generator uniquely allows platform-independent application spec that can be instantiated into multiple environments without specification changes.

metric_value: any-language object sharing via common pipe
notes: SOM toolkit allows objects created in one language to be accessed transparently from another — key cross-language integration capability.

metric_value: included but declining
notes: OpenDoc compound document architecture cited as flexibility feature; Apple abandoned OpenDoc in 1997.

metric_value: IBM installed base with COBOL/3GL legacy; migrating to network computing
notes: Aberdeen: IBM AD tools "particularly effective in allowing IBM users to move their present applications towards network computing."

metric_value: leverage IBM network-computing ecosystem; not standalone tech
notes: Aberdeen: "IS buyers should consider IBM AD tools carefully, not so much for their standalone technology as for their ability to leverage IBM's overall network-computing capabilities."

metric_value: key to IBM network-computing success
notes: Aberdeen: "IBM will move aggressively to deliver the key VisualAge for Java toolset." Confirmed — IBM did prioritize Java tooling, eventually creating Eclipse.

metric_value: needs cross-platform partitioning repository and auto-deployment
notes: Aberdeen: IBM will "beef up VisualAge Generator with cross-platform application-partitioning-supporting repository and automated-deployment features."

metric_value: improved productivity and cost savings vs CSP
notes: Customer Amerex cited VisualAge Generator for rapid migration from CSP (IBM Cross System Product) with improved productivity in manufacturing apps.

metric_value: application-performance scalability achieved
notes: Customer ImageScan cited VisualAge for C++ performance scalability and OOP+procedural code combination for check processing.

metric_value: discontinued April 30 2007
notes: IBM discontinued the VisualAge brand family April 30, 2007. Most products replaced by WebSphere Studio / Rational Application Developer / Eclipse-based tools. Source: Wikipedia VisualAge.

metric_value: VisualAge Micro Edition evolved into Eclipse; donated to open source 2001
notes: VisualAge Micro Edition (embedded Java development) was reimplemented in Java and became the Eclipse Framework. IBM donated Eclipse to open source in 2001 — became world's dominant Java IDE. Source: Wikipedia VisualAge.

metric_value: transferred 2005 as VA Smalltalk
notes: IBM transferred VisualAge Smalltalk to Instantiations in 2005 as VA Smalltalk (VAST Platform). Still maintained today. Source: Instantiations.com.

metric_value: discontinued; CORBA and J2EE won enterprise middleware
notes: IBM SOM/DSOM was discontinued. Microsoft DCOM won desktop objects; CORBA/J2EE/Web Services won enterprise. OpenDoc (also cited) was abandoned by Apple in 1997 and IBM shortly after.

metric_value: evolved to IBM WebSphere Business Process Manager
notes: FlowMark evolved through IBM MQ Workflow to IBM WebSphere Business Process Manager; workflow/BPM became a major enterprise software category.

metric_value: scalability + flexibility + lifecycle + new-tech support
notes: Aberdeen framework: 4 key criteria for evaluating network computing development tools: scalability, open flexibility, lifecycle support, new-tech (Java/distributed objects) support.

metric_name: IBM Extended Transaction Services Internet scaling
metric_value: comm multiplexing and load-balancing for web bottlenecks
notes: IBM model allows Java applet to bypass web server and connect directly to network server via TCP/IP — addressing key 1997 web server bottleneck.

metric_value: Smalltalk skills transferable to Java
notes: Aberdeen notes VisualAge Smalltalk clients targeted to be interoperable with future VisualAge Java server software — migration path for Smalltalk users.
